input.background: A particular random sample of $n$ observations can be used to calculate a sample proportion. The count of successes in the sample will vary according to a probability distribution. When the sample is large, the number of successes will vary according to a Normal distribution. At the same time, the sampling distribution of proportion $\hat{p}$ in large samples will be Normally distributed.
input.question: The sampling distribution of proportion $\hat{p}$ in large samples will be Normally distributed with mean $p$ and standard deviation equal to (e) $\_\_\_\_$. What is the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$?

n.description: The number of observations in the sample

p.description: The true proportion of successes in the population

hat_p.description: The proportion of successes in the sample

standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the sample proportion

meta_info.caption: Fill in the blanks.
meta_info.section: 8.3 SAMPLING BEHAVIOR OF A COUNT AND PROPORTION
